stage.subtitle_ar: تعلَّمي كيف يعمل المبرمجون معًا: التنسيق داخل الفريق، وإدارة الإصدارات عبر Git، ومراجعة الكود بأسلوب أجايل.

content.intro_ar: في عالم البرمجة، نادرًا ما يبني شخص واحد التطبيق بمفرده؛ فالعمل الحقيقي يجري داخل فريق. في تخصّصكِ الجامعي ستكتبين الكود مع زملاء، وتتشاركين المهام، وتتواصلين معهم بالإنجليزية كل يوم. في هذا الدرس ستتعلّمين كلمات وعبارات الفريق البرمجي: الأدوار، والمهام، والمواعيد النهائية، والاجتماعات، والتغذية الراجعة.

vocab.meaning_en: a group of people who work together toward the same goal
vocab.example: Our team builds a new app every semester.

vocab.meaning_en: a person who writes and builds software
vocab.example: Each developer works on a different part of the app.

vocab.meaning_en: a piece of work that someone must do
vocab.example: My first task was to design the login page.

vocab.meaning_en: the time by which a task must be finished
vocab.example: The deadline for the project is next Friday.

vocab.meaning_en: a time when people come together to talk about work
vocab.example: We have a short meeting every morning.

vocab.meaning_en: a duty or task that you must take care of
vocab.example: Testing the code is her responsibility.

vocab.meaning_en: to share information with other people
vocab.example: Good teams communicate clearly and often.

vocab.meaning_en: a planned piece of work with a clear goal
vocab.example: The whole project must be ready in three weeks.

vocab.meaning_en: another member of your team
vocab.example: I asked a teammate to help me fix the error.

vocab.meaning_en: the movement toward finishing a goal
vocab.example: We check our progress at the end of the day.

vocab.meaning_en: the job or part that a person plays in a team
vocab.example: Her role is to design the user interface.

vocab.meaning_en: comments that help you improve your work
vocab.example: The feedback from my teammate made my code cleaner.

paragraphs: Software is almost never built by one person alone. Most programs are made by a team of developers who share the same goal. Each member has a clear role and a set of responsibilities. When everyone knows their part, the whole project moves forward more smoothly.

A big project is divided into smaller tasks. The team meets to decide who will do each task and to set a deadline for it. During a short meeting, teammates talk about what they finished and what they will do next. This helps everyone see the progress and notice problems early.

Good teams communicate clearly and often. If you are stuck, you should tell your teammates instead of staying silent for days. When someone reviews your work, they give feedback that helps you improve. Feedback is not an attack on you as a person; it is a normal part of building good software.

Working in a team also means being reliable. If you promise to finish a task by a certain deadline, others may be waiting for it before they can start their own work. When each developer keeps their promises and shares information, the team becomes stronger than any single person.

key_phrases.en: I'll take care of this task.
Can you help me with this part?
key_phrases.ar: هل بالإمكان مساعدتي في هذا الجزء؟

key_phrases.en: I finished my task on time.
Let's discuss this in the meeting.

comprehension.question: How is most software built?
comprehension.explanation_ar: أغلب البرامج يبنيها فريق من المطوّرين يتشاركون الهدف نفسه، لا شخص واحد بمفرده.

comprehension.question: Why does a team divide a big project into smaller tasks?
options: So members can share the work and set deadlines
comprehension.correct: So members can share the work and set deadlines
comprehension.explanation_ar: تقسيم المشروع إلى مهام صغيرة يتيح توزيع العمل وتحديد موعد نهائي لكل مهمّة.

comprehension.question: What is feedback in a good team?
options: A normal part of building good software
A reason to leave the team
comprehension.correct: A normal part of building good software
comprehension.explanation_ar: التغذية الراجعة ليست هجومًا شخصيًا، بل جزء طبيعي يساعدكِ على تحسين عملكِ.

comprehension.question: What should you do if you are stuck on a task?
options: Wait for the deadline to pass
comprehension.explanation_ar: من الأفضل إخبار زملائكِ عندما تتعثّرين بدلًا من البقاء صامتة لأيام.

comprehension.question: Why is being reliable important in a team?
options: Because others may be waiting for your work
comprehension.correct: Because others may be waiting for your work
comprehension.explanation_ar: قد ينتظر بقية الفريق إنجازكِ لمهمّتكِ قبل أن يبدؤوا عملهم، لذلك الالتزام مهمّ.

comprehension.question: What does a 'role' mean in a team?
options: The part or job a person plays
comprehension.correct: The part or job a person plays
comprehension.explanation_ar: الدور هو الجزء أو المهمّة التي يؤدّيها الشخص داخل الفريق.

task.prompt_en: Write 4–5 sentences about a time you worked in a team (at school or online). What was your role, and how did you communicate with the others?
task.prompt_ar: اكتبي ٤–٥ جُمل عن مرّة عملتِ فيها ضمن فريق (في المدرسة أو عبر الإنترنت). ما كان دوركِ، وكيف تواصلتِ مع الآخرين؟
task.guidance_ar: استخدمي ٣ كلمات على الأقل من كلمات الدرس، مثل team وrole وfeedback.

content.intro_ar: عندما يكتب فريق كامل الكود معًا، يحتاجون إلى طريقة آمنة لحفظ عملهم ومشاركته دون أن يفسد أحدهم عمل الآخر. هنا يأتي دور Git، وهو الأداة التي يستخدمها كل مبرمج تقريبًا في العالم. في هذا الدرس ستتعرّفين على مصطلحات Git بالإنجليزية: المستودع، والإيداع، والفروع، والدمج، والرفع والسحب — وهي كلمات ستسمعينها كل يوم في مجال البرمجة.

vocab.meaning_en: a place where all the files and history of a project are stored
vocab.example: All our code lives in one repository.

vocab.meaning_en: a saved change with a message that describes it
vocab.example: I made a commit after fixing the bug.

vocab.meaning_en: a separate copy of the code where you can work safely
vocab.example: She created a branch for the new feature.

vocab.meaning_en: to join two branches of code together
vocab.example: We merge the branch after the tests pass.

vocab.meaning_en: to copy a repository onto your own computer
vocab.example: First, clone the repository to your laptop.

vocab.meaning_en: to send your commits to the shared repository
vocab.example: Don't forget to push your changes tonight.

vocab.meaning_en: to bring the latest changes from the shared repository to your computer
vocab.example: I pull every morning before I start working.

vocab.meaning_en: one saved state of the code at a point in time
vocab.example: We went back to an older version of the app.

vocab.meaning_en: when two changes touch the same line and Git cannot choose
vocab.example: We had a conflict because we edited the same line.

vocab.meaning_en: the full record of every change made to the code
vocab.example: The history shows who changed each file.

vocab.meaning_en: the shared copy of the repository stored online
vocab.example: Everyone pushes their work to the remote.

vocab.meaning_en: a safe extra copy of your work
vocab.example: Git also works as a backup of your code.

reading.title_en: Git: A Time Machine for Code
paragraphs: When a team writes code together, they need a safe way to save and share their work. This is why almost every software team uses Git. Git is a tool that keeps the full history of a project. Every time you save a change, you make a commit with a short message that explains what you did.

All the files and their history live in a repository. The shared copy that everyone uses is stored online and is called the remote. To start working, you clone the repository onto your own computer. When you finish some work, you push your commits to the remote so your teammates can see them. Before you start a new task, you pull the latest changes so your copy stays up to date.

To work safely, you create a branch. A branch is a separate copy of the code where you can try new ideas without breaking the main version. When your work is ready and tested, you merge your branch back into the main code. Sometimes two people change the same line, and Git cannot decide which change to keep. This is called a conflict, and the team must fix it by hand.

Because Git stores every version, it also works like a backup and a time machine. If a new change breaks the program, you can look at the history and go back to an earlier version. This makes teamwork safer and far less stressful.

key_phrases.en: Let me pull the latest changes.
I created a new branch for this feature.

comprehension.question: What does Git keep for a project?
comprehension.explanation_ar: يحفظ Git السجلّ الكامل لكل تغيير جرى على الكود، وليس الملف الأحدث فقط.

comprehension.question: What is a commit?
options: A saved change with a short message
comprehension.correct: A saved change with a short message
comprehension.explanation_ar: الإيداع (commit) هو تغيير محفوظ مرفق برسالة قصيرة تشرح ما فعلتِه.

comprehension.question: What do you do to copy a repository onto your own computer?
comprehension.explanation_ar: الاستنساخ (clone) هو نسخ المستودع إلى جهازكِ لتبدئي العمل عليه.

comprehension.question: Why do developers create a branch?
options: To break the main code on purpose
To work safely without breaking the main version
comprehension.correct: To work safely without breaking the main version
comprehension.explanation_ar: الفرع (branch) نسخة منفصلة تتيح تجربة أفكار جديدة دون التأثير على النسخة الرئيسة.

comprehension.question: What is a conflict in Git?
options: A meeting that is too long
When two changes touch the same line
A new developer on the team
comprehension.correct: When two changes touch the same line
comprehension.explanation_ar: يحدث التعارض عندما يعدّل شخصان السطر نفسه، فلا يستطيع Git اختيار أيّ التغييرين يبقى.

comprehension.question: How is Git like a time machine?
options: It lets you go back to an earlier version
It writes the code for you
comprehension.correct: It lets you go back to an earlier version
comprehension.explanation_ar: لأن Git يحفظ كل إصدار، يمكنكِ الرجوع إلى نسخة سابقة إذا كسر تغييرٌ جديد البرنامج.

task.prompt_en: Imagine you are starting a small coding project with a friend. Write 4–5 sentences explaining how you would use Git to work together safely.
task.prompt_ar: تخيّلي أنكِ تبدئين مشروعًا برمجيًا صغيرًا مع صديقة. اكتبي ٤–٥ جُمل تشرحين فيها كيف ستستخدمان Git للعمل معًا بأمان.
task.guidance_ar: استخدمي ٣ كلمات على الأقل من كلمات الدرس، مثل repository وcommit وbranch.

content.intro_ar: لا تكتفي الفرق الحديثة بكتابة الكود؛ بل تتّبع طريقة عمل منظّمة تُسمّى «أجايل» (Agile)، وتراجع كود بعضها بعضًا قبل قبوله. هاتان الممارستان ستقابلينهما في أول وظيفة تقنية لكِ. في هذا الدرس ستتعلّمين مفردات السبرنت، والاجتماع اليومي، وقائمة المهام، وطلب الدمج، والمراجعة، والموافقة — وكلها تساعد الفريق على بناء برمجيات عالية الجودة بسرعة وأمان.

vocab.meaning_en: a flexible way of building software in small, quick steps
vocab.example: Our team uses an agile way of working.

vocab.meaning_en: a short period, often two weeks, to finish a set of tasks
vocab.example: We chose five tasks for this sprint.

vocab.meaning_en: a careful check of someone's work before it is accepted
vocab.example: Every change gets a review before it is merged.

vocab.meaning_en: a request to add your branch's changes into the main code
vocab.example: She opened a pull request for her new feature.

vocab.meaning_en: the person who reads and checks the code
vocab.example: The reviewer found a small mistake in my code.

vocab.meaning_en: a note that explains code or suggests a change
vocab.example: He left a helpful comment on line ten.

vocab.meaning_en: to accept the changes so they can be merged
vocab.example: Two people must approve the pull request.

vocab.meaning_en: a short daily meeting where the team shares progress
vocab.example: Our standup takes only ten minutes.

vocab.meaning_en: the list of tasks and features still to be done
vocab.example: We added the new idea to the backlog.

vocab.meaning_en: one full cycle of building, testing, and improving
vocab.example: Each iteration makes the product a little better.

vocab.meaning_en: how good and reliable the software is
vocab.example: Code review protects the quality of the app.

vocab.meaning_en: a mistake in the code that makes it behave wrongly
vocab.example: The reviewer caught a bug before users saw it.

paragraphs: Software needs change quickly, so many teams work in a flexible way called agile. Instead of planning everything for a whole year, an agile team builds software in small steps. They keep a backlog, which is a list of all the tasks and features they still need to do. From this list, they choose the most important items for the next short period of work.

This short period is often two weeks long and is called a sprint. At the start of a sprint, the team picks a set of tasks it can finish in that time. Every morning they hold a standup, a short daily meeting where each person says what they did yesterday, what they will do today, and what is blocking them. Each sprint is one iteration: the team builds, tests, and improves the product, then starts again.

Before new code becomes part of the project, it usually goes through a code review. The developer opens a pull request to ask the team to add their branch into the main code. A reviewer reads the code carefully and leaves comments to explain problems or suggest better solutions. The goal is not to blame anyone, but to protect the quality of the product and to catch any bug early.
When the code is clean and correct, the reviewer will approve the pull request, and the change is merged. Agile and code review work together to help teams build good software quickly and safely.

key_phrases.en: What will you work on today?
I left a few comments on your code.
key_phrases.ar: تركتُ بعض التعليقات على الكود الخاص بك.

key_phrases.en: This looks good to me. Approved!
key_phrases.ar: يبدو هذا جيدًا بالنسبة لي. تمّت الموافقة!

key_phrases.en: Let's add this to the backlog.
We'll finish this in the next sprint.

comprehension.question: What is agile?
options: A flexible way to build software in small steps
A single long plan for one whole year
comprehension.correct: A flexible way to build software in small steps
comprehension.explanation_ar: أجايل طريقة مرنة لبناء البرمجيات على خطوات صغيرة وسريعة بدل التخطيط لعام كامل مرّة واحدة.

comprehension.question: What is a backlog?
options: A list of tasks still to be done
comprehension.correct: A list of tasks still to be done
comprehension.explanation_ar: الـ backlog هو قائمة بكل المهام والميزات التي لم يُنجزها الفريق بعد.

comprehension.question: What happens in a standup?
options: The team writes all the code
Each person shares progress and blockers
comprehension.correct: Each person shares progress and blockers
comprehension.explanation_ar: في الاجتماع اليومي القصير يشارك كل شخص ما أنجزه وما سيعمل عليه وما الذي يعيقه.

comprehension.question: Why does a developer open a pull request?
options: To ask the team to add their branch to the main code
comprehension.correct: To ask the team to add their branch to the main code
comprehension.explanation_ar: طلب الدمج (pull request) هو طلب لإضافة تغييرات فرعكِ إلى الكود الرئيس بعد مراجعتها.

comprehension.question: What is the main goal of a code review?
options: To protect the quality and catch bugs
comprehension.correct: To protect the quality and catch bugs
comprehension.explanation_ar: هدف مراجعة الكود ليس لوم أحد، بل حماية جودة المنتج واكتشاف الأخطاء مبكرًا.

comprehension.question: What does a reviewer do when the code is clean and correct?
comprehension.explanation_ar: عندما يكون الكود سليمًا، يوافق المُراجِع على طلب الدمج ليُدمَج التغيير في الكود الرئيس.

task.prompt_en: Record a short standup update (about 30–45 seconds). Say what you did yesterday, what you will do today, and whether anything is blocking you.
task.prompt_ar: سجّلي تحديثًا قصيرًا على طريقة الـ standup (حوالي ٣٠–٤٥ ثانية). قولي ما الذي أنجزتِه بالأمس، وما الذي ستعملين عليه اليوم، وهل هناك ما يعيقكِ.
task.guidance_ar: استخدمي كلمات مثل sprint وtask وblocked، وتحدّثي بثقة وبجُمل قصيرة.
